TR_Protection+30+Chemical_Engineering_Jo.gold_answer: Modification 1: Reducing CPCM conductivity from 6 to 0.5 W/(m·K) concentrates heat around the triggering battery and impedes heat transfer through the cooling plate to adjacent batteries, extending total propagation time from 23.5 s to 112.7 s. Modification 2: Increasing coolant flow rate from 0.009 to 0.029 m/s enhances heat dissipation through the cooling plate, extending propagation time across the plate from 17.8 s to 32.9 s (1.84×). Note: increased flow rate only significantly affects batteries separated by the cooling plate; for batteries in the same row wrapped by CPCM, flow rate changes have minimal effect because CPCM thermal insulation limits heat reaching the coolant.
